title: Emergency — Monologue
description: Listen, then prove you were listening

_voiceScript.script:
Peter: Dobrý deň, volám 112. Tu je nehoda!
Dispečerka: Kde ste?
Peter: Som na Hlavnej ulici, pred bankou.
Dispečerka: Čo sa stalo?
Peter: Dve autá sa zrazili. Jeden muž je zranený.
Dispečerka: Je pri vedomí?
Peter: Áno, ale bolí ho noha.
Dispečerka: Zavolali ste políciu?
Peter: Nie. Prosím, zavolajte políciu aj záchranku!
Dispečerka: Dobre. Záchranári idú. Číslo záchranky je 155.
Peter: A hasiči? Jedno auto horí!
Dispečerka: Zavolám aj hasičov. Ich číslo je 150.
Peter: Čo mám robiť teraz?
Dispečerka: Nestojte pri autách. Čakajte v bezpečnosti.
Peter: Rozumiem. Ďakujem za pomoc!

exercises.instruction: Tap every word or expression you heard in the conversation.

questions.questionEn: The dispatcher asks what happened. What does Peter say about the cars?
questions.answerMeaning: Dve autá sa zrazili. = Two cars collided. Zraziť sa = to collide.

questions.questionEn: Peter notices something else dangerous. What does he say?
questions.answerMeaning: Jedno auto horí! = One car is on fire! Horieť = to burn / be on fire.
Peter says volám 112. In Slovakia 112 is the main emergency number.

questions.questionEn: The dispatcher asks about the injured man. What does she want to know?
questions.answerMeaning: Je pri vedomí? = Is he conscious? Pri vedomí = conscious. This is a key emergency phrase.

questions.questionEn: What two emergency numbers does the dispatcher give Peter?
What does the dispatcher tell Peter to do while he waits?

answers.textEn: Stand near the cars to help
Stay away from the cars and wait somewhere safe

questions.answerMeaning: Nestojte pri autách. Čakajte v bezpečnosti. = Do not stand near the cars. Wait somewhere safe.

sentences.promptEn: Two cars collided. One man is injured.
Please call the police and an ambulance!
The paramedics are on the way. The ambulance number is 155.
Do not stand near the cars. Wait somewhere safe.
